response:
```python
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from scipy import sparse
import harmonypy as hm

# Set up scanpy settings
sc.settings.verbosity = 3
sc.settings.set_figure_params(dpi=80, facecolor='white')

# Load the main dataset
adata = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/mouse_brain/mouse_brain.h5ad')

# Load the second slice
adata2 = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/mouse_brain/mouse_brain_slice_2.h5ad')

print(f"Slice 1 shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"Slice 2 shape: {adata2.shape}")

# Make variable names unique
adata.var_names_make_unique()
adata2.var_names_make_unique()

# Add batch labels to distinguish the slices
adata.obs['batch'] = 'slice_1'
adata2.obs['batch'] = 'slice_2'

# Concatenate the datasets
adata_combined = sc.concat([adata, adata2], axis=0, join='outer', index_unique='-')

print(f"Combined shape: {adata_combined.shape}")
print(f"Batch distribution:\n{adata_combined.obs['batch'].value_counts()}")

# Basic preprocessing
# Calculate QC metrics
adata_combined.var['mt'] = adata_combined.var_names.str.startswith('mt-')
sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata_combined, percent_top=None, log1p=False, inplace=True)

# Normalize and log transform
adata_combined.raw = adata_combined
sc.pp.normalize_total(adata_combined, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(adata_combined)

# Find highly variable genes
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata_combined, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, min_disp=0.5, batch_key='batch')
sc.pl.highly_variable_genes(adata_combined, save='_hvg_comparison.pdf')

# Keep only highly variable genes for integration
adata_combined.raw = adata_combined
adata_combined = adata_combined[:, adata_combined.var.highly_variable]

# Scale the data
sc.pp.scale(adata_combined, max_value=10)

# Perform PCA
sc.tl.pca(adata_combined, svd_solver='arpack')

# Batch correction using Harmony
# Convert to dense for Harmony
X_pca = adata_combined.obsm['X_pca']
batch_info = adata_combined.obs[['batch']].copy()

# Run Harmony
ho = hm.run_harmony(X_pca, batch_info, ['batch'], max_iter_harmony=20)
adata_combined.obsm['X_harmony'] = ho.Z_corr.T

# Compute neighborhood graph using Harmony-corrected embeddings
sc.pp.neighbors(adata_combined, use_rep='X_harmony', n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=40)

# Perform UMAP
sc.tl.umap(adata_combined)

# Leiden clustering
sc.tl.leiden(adata_combined, resolution=0.5)

# Create visualization plots
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 12))

# Plot UMAP by batch
sc.pl.umap(adata_combined, color='batch', ax=axes[0,0], show=False, frameon=False)
axes[0,0].set_title('Batch (before integration)')

# Plot UMAP by celltype if available
if 'celltype' in adata_combined.obs.columns:
    sc.pl.umap(adata_combined, color='celltype', ax=axes[0,1], show=False, frameon=False, legend_loc='on data')
    axes[0,1].set_title('Cell types')

# Plot UMAP by Leiden clusters
sc.pl.umap(adata_combined, color='leiden', ax=axes[0,2], show=False, frameon=False, legend_loc='on data')
axes[0,2].set_title('Leiden clusters')

# Spatial plots
# Extract coordinates
slice1_mask = adata_combined.obs['batch'] == 'slice_1'
slice2_mask = adata_combined.obs['batch'] == 'slice_2'

# Spatial plot for slice 1
if 'x' in adata_combined.obs.columns and 'y' in adata_combined.obs.columns:
    x1, y1 = adata_combined[slice1_mask].obs['x'], adata_combined[slice1_mask].obs['y']
    colors1 = adata_combined[slice1_mask].obs['leiden'].astype('category').cat.codes
    
    scatter1 = axes[1,0].scatter(x1, y1, c=colors1, s=1, alpha=0.8)
    axes[1,0].set_title('Slice 1 - Spatial (Leiden)')
    axes[1,0].set_aspect('equal')
    
    # Spatial plot for slice 2
    x2, y2 = adata_combined[slice2_mask].obs['x'], adata_combined[slice2_mask].obs['y']
    colors2 = adata_combined[slice2_mask].obs['leiden'].astype('category').cat.codes
    
    scatter2 = axes[1,1].scatter(x2, y2, c=colors2, s=1, alpha=0.8)
    axes[1,1].set_title('Slice 2 - Spatial (Leiden)')
    axes[1,1].set_aspect('equal')

# Plot batch mixing assessment
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score

# Calculate silhouette score for batch mixing (lower is better for integration)
batch_numeric = adata_combined.obs['batch'].astype('category').cat.codes
sil_score = silhouette_score(adata_combined.obsm['X_harmony'], batch_numeric)

axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.8, f'Harmony Integration Results:', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.7, f'Silhouette Score: {sil_score:.3f}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.6, f'(Lower values indicate better mixing)', fontsize=10)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.4, f'Total cells: {adata_combined.n_obs}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.3, f'Total genes: {adata_combined.n_vars}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.2, f'Clusters found: {len(adata_combined.obs["leiden"].unique())}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].set_xlim(0, 1)
axes[1,2].set_ylim(0, 1)
axes[1,2].axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q26/run_0/integration_comparison.pdf', 
            dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# Calculate integration metrics
print("\n" + "="*50)
print("INTEGRATION ANALYSIS SUMMARY")
print("="*50)

print(f"\nDataset Information:")
print(f"- Slice 1: {sum(slice1_mask)} cells")
print(f"- Slice 2: {sum(slice2_mask)} cells")
print(f"- Total: {adata_combined.n_obs} cells")
print(f"- Genes after filtering: {adata_combined.n_vars}")

print(f"\nIntegration Quality:")
print(f"- Silhouette score (batch): {sil_score:.3f}")
print(f"- Number of clusters: {len(adata_combined.obs['leiden'].unique())}")

# Check cluster composition across batches
cluster_batch_crosstab = pd.crosstab(adata_combined.obs['leiden'], adata_combined.obs['batch'])
print(f"\nCluster composition across batches:")
print(cluster_batch_crosstab)

# Calculate what fraction of clusters have cells from both batches
mixed_clusters = (cluster_batch_crosstab > 0).sum(axis=1) == 2
mixing_ratio = mixed_clusters.sum() / len(mixed_clusters)
print(f"\nMixed clusters (containing both batches): {mixing_ratio:.1%}")

# If celltype information is available, check preservation
if 'celltype' in adata_combined.obs.columns:
    # Check how well celltypes are preserved across batches
    celltype_batch_crosstab = pd.crosstab(adata_combined.obs['celltype'], adata_combined.obs['batch'])
    print(f"\nCelltype distribution across batches:")
    print(celltype_batch_crosstab)

print(f"\n" + "="*50)
print("INTEGRATION COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY")
print("="*50)
print("The two brain slices have been integrated using Harmony batch correction.")
print("This removes technical batch effects while preserving biological variation.")
print("The integrated data can now be used for comparative analysis between slices.")

# Save the integrated result
adata_combined.write('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q26/run_0/output.h5ad')
```

query_text: These two tissue sections are from the same brain region but look quite different. Can you make them comparable?
answer:
```python
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from scipy import sparse
import harmonypy as hm

# Set up scanpy settings
sc.settings.verbosity = 3
sc.settings.set_figure_params(dpi=80, facecolor='white')

# Load the main dataset
adata = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/mouse_brain/mouse_brain.h5ad')

# Load the second slice
adata2 = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/mouse_brain/mouse_brain_slice_2.h5ad')

print(f"Slice 1 shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"Slice 2 shape: {adata2.shape}")

# Make variable names unique
adata.var_names_make_unique()
adata2.var_names_make_unique()

# Add batch labels to distinguish the slices
adata.obs['batch'] = 'slice_1'
adata2.obs['batch'] = 'slice_2'

# Concatenate the datasets
adata_combined = sc.concat([adata, adata2], axis=0, join='outer', index_unique='-')

print(f"Combined shape: {adata_combined.shape}")
print(f"Batch distribution:\n{adata_combined.obs['batch'].value_counts()}")

# Basic preprocessing
# Calculate QC metrics
adata_combined.var['mt'] = adata_combined.var_names.str.startswith('mt-')
sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata_combined, percent_top=None, log1p=False, inplace=True)

# Normalize and log transform
adata_combined.raw = adata_combined
sc.pp.normalize_total(adata_combined, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(adata_combined)

# Find highly variable genes
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata_combined, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, min_disp=0.5, batch_key='batch')
sc.pl.highly_variable_genes(adata_combined, save='_hvg_comparison.pdf')

# Keep only highly variable genes for integration
adata_combined.raw = adata_combined
adata_combined = adata_combined[:, adata_combined.var.highly_variable]

# Scale the data
sc.pp.scale(adata_combined, max_value=10)

# Perform PCA
sc.tl.pca(adata_combined, svd_solver='arpack')

# Batch correction using Harmony
# Convert to dense for Harmony
X_pca = adata_combined.obsm['X_pca']
batch_info = adata_combined.obs[['batch']].copy()

# Run Harmony
ho = hm.run_harmony(X_pca, batch_info, ['batch'], max_iter_harmony=20)
adata_combined.obsm['X_harmony'] = ho.Z_corr.T

# Compute neighborhood graph using Harmony-corrected embeddings
sc.pp.neighbors(adata_combined, use_rep='X_harmony', n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=40)

# Perform UMAP
sc.tl.umap(adata_combined)

# Leiden clustering
sc.tl.leiden(adata_combined, resolution=0.5)

# Create visualization plots
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 12))

# Plot UMAP by batch
sc.pl.umap(adata_combined, color='batch', ax=axes[0,0], show=False, frameon=False)
axes[0,0].set_title('Batch (before integration)')

# Plot UMAP by celltype if available
if 'celltype' in adata_combined.obs.columns:
    sc.pl.umap(adata_combined, color='celltype', ax=axes[0,1], show=False, frameon=False, legend_loc='on data')
    axes[0,1].set_title('Cell types')

# Plot UMAP by Leiden clusters
sc.pl.umap(adata_combined, color='leiden', ax=axes[0,2], show=False, frameon=False, legend_loc='on data')
axes[0,2].set_title('Leiden clusters')

# Spatial plots
# Extract coordinates
slice1_mask = adata_combined.obs['batch'] == 'slice_1'
slice2_mask = adata_combined.obs['batch'] == 'slice_2'

# Spatial plot for slice 1
if 'x' in adata_combined.obs.columns and 'y' in adata_combined.obs.columns:
    x1, y1 = adata_combined[slice1_mask].obs['x'], adata_combined[slice1_mask].obs['y']
    colors1 = adata_combined[slice1_mask].obs['leiden'].astype('category').cat.codes
    
    scatter1 = axes[1,0].scatter(x1, y1, c=colors1, s=1, alpha=0.8)
    axes[1,0].set_title('Slice 1 - Spatial (Leiden)')
    axes[1,0].set_aspect('equal')
    
    # Spatial plot for slice 2
    x2, y2 = adata_combined[slice2_mask].obs['x'], adata_combined[slice2_mask].obs['y']
    colors2 = adata_combined[slice2_mask].obs['leiden'].astype('category').cat.codes
    
    scatter2 = axes[1,1].scatter(x2, y2, c=colors2, s=1, alpha=0.8)
    axes[1,1].set_title('Slice 2 - Spatial (Leiden)')
    axes[1,1].set_aspect('equal')

# Plot batch mixing assessment
from sklearn.metrics import silhouette_score

# Calculate silhouette score for batch mixing (lower is better for integration)
batch_numeric = adata_combined.obs['batch'].astype('category').cat.codes
sil_score = silhouette_score(adata_combined.obsm['X_harmony'], batch_numeric)

axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.8, f'Harmony Integration Results:', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.7, f'Silhouette Score: {sil_score:.3f}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.6, f'(Lower values indicate better mixing)', fontsize=10)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.4, f'Total cells: {adata_combined.n_obs}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.3, f'Total genes: {adata_combined.n_vars}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].text(0.1, 0.2, f'Clusters found: {len(adata_combined.obs["leiden"].unique())}', fontsize=12)
axes[1,2].set_xlim(0, 1)
axes[1,2].set_ylim(0, 1)
axes[1,2].axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q26/run_0/integration_comparison.pdf', 
            dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# Calculate integration metrics
print("\n" + "="*50)
print("INTEGRATION ANALYSIS SUMMARY")
print("="*50)

print(f"\nDataset Information:")
print(f"- Slice 1: {sum(slice1_mask)} cells")
print(f"- Slice 2: {sum(slice2_mask)} cells")
print(f"- Total: {adata_combined.n_obs} cells")
print(f"- Genes after filtering: {adata_combined.n_vars}")

print(f"\nIntegration Quality:")
print(f"- Silhouette score (batch): {sil_score:.3f}")
print(f"- Number of clusters: {len(adata_combined.obs['leiden'].unique())}")

# Check cluster composition across batches
cluster_batch_crosstab = pd.crosstab(adata_combined.obs['leiden'], adata_combined.obs['batch'])
print(f"\nCluster composition across batches:")
print(cluster_batch_crosstab)

# Calculate what fraction of clusters have cells from both batches
mixed_clusters = (cluster_batch_crosstab > 0).sum(axis=1) == 2
mixing_ratio = mixed_clusters.sum() / len(mixed_clusters)
print(f"\nMixed clusters (containing both batches): {mixing_ratio:.1%}")

# If celltype information is available, check preservation
if 'celltype' in adata_combined.obs.columns:
    # Check how well celltypes are preserved across batches
    celltype_batch_crosstab = pd.crosstab(adata_combined.obs['celltype'], adata_combined.obs['batch'])
    print(f"\nCelltype distribution across batches:")
    print(celltype_batch_crosstab)

print(f"\n" + "="*50)
print("INTEGRATION COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY")
print("="*50)
print("The two brain slices have been integrated using Harmony batch correction.")
print("This removes technical batch effects while preserving biological variation.")
print("The integrated data can now be used for comparative analysis between slices.")

# Save the integrated result
adata_combined.write('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q26/run_0/output.h5ad')
```
